```{python}
])
```

## Households by heating fuel type

Weighted distribution of New York households by primary heating fuel,
computed from the statewide ResStock sample (upgrade 0 — current HVAC).

```{python}
#| label: fig-households-by-fuel
#| fig-cap: "New York households by primary heating fuel"

FUEL_CATEGORIES = [
    "Natural Gas", "Propane", "Fuel Oil", "Heat Pump", "Resistance", "Other/None",
]
FUEL_COLORS_BAR = {
    "Natural Gas": "#68bed8",
    "Fuel Oil": "#023047",
    "Resistance": "#ffc729",
    "Propane": "#4a8fa0",
    "Heat Pump": "#fc9706",
    "Other/None": "#b0b0b0",
}
FUEL_LABEL_COLORS = {
    "Natural Gas": "#3a8fa0",
    "Fuel Oil": "#023047",
    "Resistance": "#b08e00",
    "Propane": "#2d6070",
    "Heat Pump": "#c47600",
    "Other/None": "#666666",
}

src = annual_current_state

total_w = src["weight"].sum()
fuel_weights = {
    "Natural Gas": float(src.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))["weight"].sum()),
    "Fuel Oil": float(src.filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil"))["weight"].sum()),
    "Propane": float(src.filter(pl.col("heats_with_propane"))["weight"].sum()),
    "Resistance": float(
        src.filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")["weight"].sum()
    ),
    "Heat Pump": float(
        src.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp"))["weight"].sum()
    ),
}
fuel_weights["Other/None"] = float(total_w - sum(fuel_weights.values()))

fuel_pcts = {k: round(v / total_w * 100) for k, v in fuel_weights.items()}

fuel_df = pl.DataFrame({
    "fuel": FUEL_CATEGORIES,
    "pct": [fuel_pcts[c] for c in FUEL_CATEGORIES],
}).with_columns(pl.col("fuel").cast(pl.Enum(FUEL_CATEGORIES)))

cum = 0.0
midpoints: list[float] = []
for row_pct in fuel_df["pct"].to_list():
    midpoints.append(cum + row_pct / 2)
    cum += row_pct

label_specs = [
    {"cat": c, "mid": m, "h": h, "text": t, "sz": sz}
    for c, m, h, t, sz in [
        ("Natural Gas", midpoints[0], 1.35, f"{fuel_pcts['Natural Gas']}%\nNatural Gas", 9),
        ("Propane",     midpoints[1], 1.60, f"{fuel_pcts['Propane']}% Propane",           7),
        ("Fuel Oil",    midpoints[2], 1.38, f"{fuel_pcts['Fuel Oil']}%\nFuel Oil",       9),
        ("Heat Pump",   midpoints[3], 1.38, f"{fuel_pcts['Heat Pump']}% Heat Pump",      7),
        ("Resistance",  midpoints[4], 1.35, f"{fuel_pcts['Resistance']}%\nResistance",   9),
        ("Other/None",  midpoints[5], 1.60, f"{fuel_pcts['Other/None']}% Other",          7),
    ]
]

fuel_annotations = [
    annotate(
        "text", x=s["h"], y=s["mid"], label=s["text"],
        color=FUEL_LABEL_COLORS[s["cat"]], fontweight="bold",
        size=s["sz"], ha="center", va="bottom",
    )
    for s in label_specs
]

p_fuel = (
    ggplot(fuel_df, aes(x="'Households'", y="pct", fill="fuel"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.45)
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_fill_manual(values=FUEL_COLORS_BAR, breaks=FUEL_CATEGORIES)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0, 0))
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.5, 0, 2.5))
    + labs(x="", y="", fill="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(17.5, 4.4),
        axis_text=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks=element_blank(),
        axis_line=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
        legend_position="none",
    )
)
for a in fuel_annotations:
    p_fuel = p_fuel + a
p_fuel
```


## Bill Alignment Test (BAT) results

The master BAT table contains per-building annual bill alignment values
decomposed into delivery, supply, and total components. A positive
`BAT_percustomer` value means the customer's bill exceeds their marginal cost
of service — they cross-subsidize other customers. A negative value means they
pay less than their cost — others cross-subsidize them.

We look at all customers under current rates (upgrade 0 / run 1+2) to quantify
how current rate structures misalign bills with cost of service.
`BAT_percustomer_total` is the sum of delivery and supply components. The
unified histograms show the full population; the faceted versions break the
distribution down by heating type (fossil fuel, electric resistance, heat pump).

```{python}
#| label: bat-ingest

bat_12 = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_12, hive_partitioning=True)

bat_all = (
    bat_12
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight",
        "BAT_percustomer_total",
        HEATING_TYPE_COL,
        "sb.electric_utility",
    )
    .collect()
)
```

### Cross-subsidy example: two typical households

To make the concept of bill alignment concrete before diving into the full
distribution, we pick two individual ConEd buildings — one gas-heated, one
heat-pump-heated — whose BAT values fall near the weighted median for their
group. For each, we compare what they pay (their annual electric bill) against
what it costs to serve them (marginal cost + residual allocation).

```{python}
#| label: cross-subsidy-example-data

from plotnine import geom_segment, scale_color_identity

bldg_bat_detail = (
    bat_12
    .filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY)
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight",
        HEATING_TYPE_COL,
        "economic_burden_delivery", "economic_burden_supply",
        "residual_share_delivery", "residual_share_supply",
        "annual_bill_delivery", "annual_bill_supply",
        "BAT_percustomer_total",
    )
    .collect()
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("economic_burden_supply")).alias("mc_total"),
        (pl.col("residual_share_delivery") + pl.col("residual_share_supply")).alias("residual_total"),
        (pl.col("annual_bill_delivery") + pl.col("annual_bill_supply")).alias("revenue_total"),
    )
)

natgas = bldg_bat_detail.filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "fossil_fuel")
natgas_med = _weighted_percentile(natgas, "BAT_percustomer_total", "weight", 0.5)
natgas_pick = (
    natgas.with_columns((pl.col("BAT_percustomer_total") - natgas_med).abs().alias("_d"))
    .sort("_d").head(1)
)

hp = bldg_bat_detail.filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "heat_pump")
hp_med = _weighted_percentile(hp, "BAT_percustomer_total", "weight", 0.5)
hp_pick = (
    hp.with_columns((pl.col("BAT_percustomer_total") - hp_med).abs().alias("_d"))
    .sort("_d").head(1)
)

natgas_row = natgas_pick.row(0, named=True)
hp_row = hp_pick.row(0, named=True)

print(
    f"Gas home (bldg {natgas_row[BLDG_ID]}): "
    f"bill=${natgas_row['revenue_total']:,.0f}, "
    f"MC=${natgas_row['mc_total']:,.0f}, "
    f"residual=${natgas_row['residual_total']:,.0f}, "
    f"BAT=${natgas_row['BAT_percustomer_total']:,.0f}"
)
print(
    f"HP home  (bldg {hp_row[BLDG_ID]}): "
    f"bill=${hp_row['revenue_total']:,.0f}, "
    f"MC=${hp_row['mc_total']:,.0f}, "
    f"residual=${hp_row['residual_total']:,.0f}, "
    f"BAT=${hp_row['BAT_percustomer_total']:,.0f}"
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-cross-subsidy-example
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill vs. cost of service for two typical ConEd households. The gap between bars is the cross-subsidy."

CS_COMP_ORDER = ["Annual Bill", "Marginal Cost", "Residual Cost"]
CS_COMP_COLORS = {
    "Annual Bill": "#56B4E9",
    "Marginal Cost": "#1b7837",
    "Residual Cost": "#a6dba0",
}
CS_BAR_ORDER = ["Annual\nBill", "Cost of\nService"]
CS_HH_ORDER = ["Gas-heated home", "Heat pump home"]


def _bldg_records(row: dict, label: str) -> list[dict]:
    return [
        {"household": label, "bar": "Annual\nBill", "component": "Annual Bill",
         "dollars": row["revenue_total"]},
        {"household": label, "bar": "Cost of\nService", "component": "Marginal Cost",
         "dollars": row["mc_total"]},
        {"household": label, "bar": "Cost of\nService", "component": "Residual Cost",
         "dollars": row["residual_total"]},
    ]


cs_records: list[dict] = []
cs_records.extend(_bldg_records(natgas_row, "Gas-heated home"))
cs_records.extend(_bldg_records(hp_row, "Heat pump home"))

cs_plot_df = pl.DataFrame(cs_records).with_columns(
    pl.col("household").cast(pl.Enum(CS_HH_ORDER)),
    pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(CS_BAR_ORDER)),
    pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(CS_COMP_ORDER)),
)

cs_bar_totals = (
    cs_plot_df.group_by("household", "bar")
    .agg(pl.col("dollars").sum().alias("total"))
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("household").cast(pl.Enum(CS_HH_ORDER)),
        pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(CS_BAR_ORDER)),
        pl.col("total")
        .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}", return_dtype=pl.String)
        .alias("total_label"),
    )
)

natgas_bill = natgas_row["revenue_total"]
natgas_cost = natgas_row["mc_total"] + natgas_row["residual_total"]
natgas_bat = natgas_row["BAT_percustomer_total"]

hp_bill = hp_row["revenue_total"]
hp_cost = hp_row["mc_total"] + hp_row["residual_total"]
hp_bat = hp_row["BAT_percustomer_total"]

bracket_df = pl.DataFrame([
    {"household": "Gas-heated home", "x": 1.0, "xend": 2.0,
     "y": natgas_bill, "yend": natgas_cost, "clr": "#1b5e20"},
    {"household": "Heat pump home", "x": 1.0, "xend": 2.0,
     "y": hp_bill, "yend": hp_cost, "clr": "#b71c1c"},
]).with_columns(pl.col("household").cast(pl.Enum(CS_HH_ORDER)))

bat_labels = pl.DataFrame([
    {"household": "Gas-heated home", "x": 1.5,
     "y": max(natgas_bill, natgas_cost) + 80,
     "label": f"Underpays\n${abs(natgas_bat):,.0f}/yr", "clr": "#1b5e20"},
    {"household": "Heat pump home", "x": 1.5,
     "y": max(hp_bill, hp_cost) + 80,
     "label": f"Overpays\n${abs(hp_bat):,.0f}/yr", "clr": "#b71c1c"},
]).with_columns(pl.col("household").cast(pl.Enum(CS_HH_ORDER)))

(
    ggplot(cs_plot_df, aes(x="bar", y="dollars", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.55)
    + geom_text(
        cs_bar_totals, aes(x="bar", y="total", label="total_label"),
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=9, nudge_y=40, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_segment(
        bracket_df,
        aes(x="x", xend="xend", y="y", yend="yend", color="clr"),
        size=1.2, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_text(
        bat_labels, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label", color="clr"),
        size=9, fontweight="bold", inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=CS_COMP_COLORS, breaks=CS_COMP_ORDER)
    + scale_color_identity()
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
    + facet_wrap("household", ncol=2)
    + labs(
        x="", y="$ / year",
        title="Annual bill vs. cost of service — two typical ConEd households",
        fill="",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 6))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: bat-histogram-helpers

from plotnine import facet_wrap

BAT_BIN_WIDTH = 50
BAT_COLORS = {"overpaying": "#b71c1c", "underpaying": "#1b5e20"}


def plot_bat_histogram(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    col: str = "BAT_percustomer_total",
    title: str = "BAT per-customer (total): HP households under current rates",
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of BAT per-customer values."""
    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(df, col, "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(df, col, "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col(col) / BAT_BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BAT_BIN_WIDTH + BAT_BIN_WIDTH / 2)
            .alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") < 0)
            .then(pl.lit("underpaying"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("overpaying"))
            .alias("sign"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "sign")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    sign_dtype = pl.Enum(["underpaying", "overpaying"])
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("sign").cast(sign_dtype))

    total_weight = df["weight"].sum()
    pct_over = df.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / total_weight * 100
    pct_under = 100 - pct_over
    w_mean = _weighted_mean(df, col, "weight")

    max_weight = cast(float, plot_df["weight_sum"].max()) if not plot_df.is_empty() else 0.0
    y_annot = max_weight * 1.08

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="sign"))
        + geom_col(width=BAT_BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="solid", color="gray", size=0.8)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=w_mean, linetype="dashed", color="#333333", size=0.7)
        + annotate(
            "text", x=w_mean, y=y_annot,
            label=f"mean: ${w_mean:,.0f}",
            ha="left" if w_mean >= 0 else "right",
            size=9, color="#333333",
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BAT_COLORS)
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + labs(x="BAT per-customer ($/year)", y="# of Homes (weighted)", title=title)
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(14, 6))
    )

    if pct_under >= 1:
        p = p + annotate(
            "text", x=(x_lo + min(0, x_hi)) / 2, y=y_annot,
            label=f"Underpaying\n{pct_under:.0f}%",
            ha="center", size=10, color="#1b5e20",
        )
    if pct_over >= 1:
        p = p + annotate(
            "text", x=(max(0, x_lo) + x_hi) / 2, y=y_annot,
            label=f"Overpaying\n{pct_over:.0f}%",
            ha="center", size=10, color="#b71c1c",
        )

    return p


HEATING_TYPE_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "fossil_fuel": "Fossil fuel",
    "electrical_resistance": "Electric resistance",
    "heat_pump": "Heat pump",
}
FACET_ORDER = ["Electric resistance", "Heat pump", "Fossil fuel", "Other"]


def plot_bat_histogram_faceted(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    col: str = "BAT_percustomer_total",
    title: str = "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type",
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of BAT per-customer, vertically faceted by heating type."""
    facet_dtype = pl.Enum(FACET_ORDER)
    labeled = df.with_columns(
        pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
        .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
        .fill_null("Other")
        .cast(facet_dtype)
        .alias("heating_label"),
    )

    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(labeled, col, "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(labeled, col, "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

    binned = (
        labeled.with_columns(
            ((pl.col(col) / BAT_BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BAT_BIN_WIDTH + BAT_BIN_WIDTH / 2)
            .alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") < 0)
            .then(pl.lit("underpaying"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("overpaying"))
            .alias("sign"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "sign", "heating_label")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    sign_dtype = pl.Enum(["underpaying", "overpaying"])
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("sign").cast(sign_dtype))

    stat_rows = []
    mean_rows = []
    for ht in [f for f in FACET_ORDER if f in labeled["heating_label"].unique().to_list()]:
        sub = labeled.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
        tw = sub["weight"].sum()
        pct_over = sub.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / tw * 100
        w_mean = _weighted_mean(sub, col, "weight")
        facet_bins = plot_df.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
        y_max = cast(float, facet_bins["weight_sum"].max()) if not facet_bins.is_empty() else 0.0
        stat_rows.append({
            "heating_label": ht,
            "x": x_hi,
            "y": y_max * 0.92,
            "label": f"mean: ${w_mean:,.0f}  ·  {pct_over:.0f}% overpaying",
        })
        mean_rows.append({"heating_label": ht, "mean_x": w_mean})

    stats_df = pl.DataFrame(stat_rows).with_columns(pl.col("heating_label").cast(facet_dtype))
    means_df = pl.DataFrame(mean_rows).with_columns(pl.col("heating_label").cast(facet_dtype))
    n_facets = labeled["heating_label"].n_unique()

    return (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="sign"))
        + geom_col(width=BAT_BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="solid", color="gray", size=0.8)
        + geom_vline(
            data=means_df, mapping=aes(xintercept="mean_x"),
            linetype="dashed", color="#333333", size=0.7,
        )
        + geom_text(
            stats_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label"),
            ha="right", size=8, color="#333333",
            inherit_aes=False,
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BAT_COLORS)
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + facet_wrap("heating_label", ncol=1, scales="free_y")
        + labs(x="BAT per-customer ($/year)", y="# of Homes (weighted)", title=title)
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(14, 3 + 3 * n_facets))
    )
```

### ConEd

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-coned
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, ConEd"

bat_coned = bat_all.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY)
plot_bat_histogram(bat_coned, title="BAT per-customer (total) — ConEd")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-coned-faceted
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_coned, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd")
```

### Statewide

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-state
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, Statewide"

plot_bat_histogram(bat_all, title="BAT per-customer (total) — Statewide")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-state-faceted
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_all, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide")
```

### Summary statistics

```{python}
#| label: bat-summary-stats

col = "BAT_percustomer_total"
facet_dtype = pl.Enum(FACET_ORDER)

labeled_all = bat_all.with_columns(
    pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
    .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
    .fill_null("Other")
    .cast(facet_dtype)
    .alias("heating_label"),
)

total_weight = labeled_all["weight"].sum()

summary_rows = []
for ht in FACET_ORDER:
    sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    w = sub["weight"].sum()
    mean_bat = _weighted_mean(sub, col, "weight")
    pct_over = sub.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / w * 100
    summary_rows.append({
        "heating_type": ht,
        "mean_bat": mean_bat,
        "pct_overpaying": pct_over / 100,
        "pct_of_households": w / total_weight,
    })

(
    GT(pl.DataFrame(summary_rows))
    .fmt_currency(columns="mean_bat", decimals=0)
    .fmt_percent(columns=["pct_overpaying", "pct_of_households"], decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        heating_type="Heating Type",
        mean_bat="Mean BAT ($/yr)",
        pct_overpaying="% Overpaying",
        pct_of_households="% of Households",
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: bat-dollar-totals

dollar_rows = []
for ht in FACET_ORDER:
    sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    weighted_bat = sub[col] * sub["weight"]
    total_over = weighted_bat.filter(weighted_bat >= 0).sum()
    total_under = weighted_bat.filter(weighted_bat < 0).sum()
    dollar_rows.append({
        "heating_type": ht,
        "total_overpayment": total_over,
        "total_underpayment": total_under,
        "net": total_over + total_under,
    })

(
    GT(pl.DataFrame(dollar_rows))
    .fmt_currency(columns=["total_overpayment", "total_underpayment", "net"], decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        heating_type="Heating Type",
        total_overpayment="Total Overpayment",
        total_underpayment="Total Underpayment",
        net="Net",
    )
)
```

### Impact of tech-specific rates on fossil fuel cross-subsidization

What happens to fossil fuel customers' cross-subsidization if tech-specific rates
zero out the net overpayment of HP customers, ER customers, or both? We spread the
released overpayment uniformly across fossil fuel households and report the shift
in their net, mean annual BAT, and mean monthly BAT (over a 6-month heating
season).

```{python}
#| label: bat-scenario-table

HEATING_MONTHS = 6

ff = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Fossil fuel")
ff_weight = ff["weight"].sum()
ff_mean_before = _weighted_mean(ff, col, "weight")
ff_net_before = (ff[col] * ff["weight"]).sum()

er = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Electric resistance")
er_net = (er[col] * er["weight"]).sum()

hp = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Heat pump")
hp_net = (hp[col] * hp["weight"]).sum()

scenarios = [
    ("HP rate only", hp_net),
    ("ER rate only", er_net),
    ("HP + ER rates", hp_net + er_net),
]

scenario_rows = []
for name, released in scenarios:
    shift = released / ff_weight
    new_net = ff_net_before + released
    new_mean = ff_mean_before + shift
    scenario_rows.append({
        "scenario": name,
        "cross_subsidy_removed": released / 1e6,
        "ff_net_after": new_net / 1e6,
        "ff_mean_bat_before": ff_mean_before,
        "ff_mean_bat_after": new_mean,
        "monthly_heating": shift / HEATING_MONTHS,
    })

(
    GT(pl.DataFrame(scenario_rows))
    .fmt_currency(columns="cross_subsidy_removed", decimals=0, pattern="{x}M")
    .fmt_currency(columns="ff_net_after", decimals=0, pattern="{x}M")
    .fmt_currency(columns=["ff_mean_bat_before", "ff_mean_bat_after"], decimals=0, pattern="{x}/yr")
    .fmt_currency(columns="monthly_heating", decimals=0, pattern="{x}/mo")
    .cols_label(
        scenario="Scenario",
        cross_subsidy_removed="Cross-Subsidy Removed",
        ff_net_after="FF Net After",
        ff_mean_bat_before="FF Mean BAT Before",
        ff_mean_bat_after="FF Mean BAT After",
        monthly_heating="Monthly (Heating)",
    )
)
```

### Typical HP customer: bill vs. cost of service by utility

The ConEd example above is illustrative, but does the pattern hold statewide?
For each utility we pick the HP building nearest the weighted-median BAT and
compare its annual electric bill against its cost of service (marginal cost +
residual allocation). The overpayment gap is consistent across all seven
service territories.

```{python}
#| label: cross-subsidy-by-utility-data

UTIL_DISPLAY: dict[str, str] = {
    "coned": "ConEd",
    "nimo": "NiMo",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "cenhud": "CenHud",
    "rge": "RG&E",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
}

hp_all_utils = (
    bat_12
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "heat_pump")
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight", "sb.electric_utility",
        "economic_burden_delivery", "economic_burden_supply",
        "residual_share_delivery", "residual_share_supply",
        "annual_bill_delivery", "annual_bill_supply",
        "BAT_percustomer_total",
    )
    .collect()
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("economic_burden_supply")).alias("mc_total"),
        (pl.col("residual_share_delivery") + pl.col("residual_share_supply")).alias("residual_total"),
        (pl.col("annual_bill_delivery") + pl.col("annual_bill_supply")).alias("revenue_total"),
    )
)

utility_picks: list[pl.DataFrame] = []
for util_code in sorted(hp_all_utils["sb.electric_utility"].unique().to_list()):
    sub = hp_all_utils.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == util_code)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    med = _weighted_percentile(sub, "BAT_percustomer_total", "weight", 0.5)
    pick = (
        sub.with_columns((pl.col("BAT_percustomer_total") - med).abs().alias("_d"))
        .sort("_d").head(1)
    )
    utility_picks.append(pick)

median_hp_bldgs = pl.concat(utility_picks)

(
    GT(median_hp_bldgs.select(
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility").replace_strict(UTIL_DISPLAY).alias("utility"),
        "revenue_total", "mc_total", "residual_total", "BAT_percustomer_total",
    ))
    .fmt_currency(
        columns=["revenue_total", "mc_total", "residual_total", "BAT_percustomer_total"],
        decimals=0,
    )
    .cols_label(
        utility="Utility",
        revenue_total="Annual Bill",
        mc_total="Marginal Cost",
        residual_total="Residual Cost",
        BAT_percustomer_total="BAT ($/yr)",
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-cross-subsidy-by-utility
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill vs. cost of service for the median HP customer in each utility. The gap between bars shows how much typical HP customers overpay."

util_order = (
    median_hp_bldgs
    .with_columns(pl.col("sb.electric_utility").replace_strict(UTIL_DISPLAY).alias("utility"))
    .sort("BAT_percustomer_total", descending=True)["utility"]
    .to_list()
)

cs_util_records: list[dict] = []
for row in median_hp_bldgs.iter_rows(named=True):
    u = UTIL_DISPLAY.get(row["sb.electric_utility"], row["sb.electric_utility"])
    cs_util_records.extend([
        {"utility": u, "bar": "Annual\nBill", "component": "Annual Bill",
         "dollars": row["revenue_total"]},
        {"utility": u, "bar": "Cost of\nService", "component": "Marginal Cost",
         "dollars": row["mc_total"]},
        {"utility": u, "bar": "Cost of\nService", "component": "Residual Cost",
         "dollars": row["residual_total"]},
    ])

cs_util_df = pl.DataFrame(cs_util_records).with_columns(
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(util_order)),
    pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(CS_BAR_ORDER)),
    pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(CS_COMP_ORDER)),
)

cs_util_totals = (
    cs_util_df.group_by("utility", "bar")
    .agg(pl.col("dollars").sum().alias("total"))
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(util_order)),
        pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(CS_BAR_ORDER)),
        pl.col("total")
        .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}", return_dtype=pl.String)
        .alias("total_label"),
    )
)

cs_bracket_rows: list[dict] = []
cs_bat_rows: list[dict] = []
for row in median_hp_bldgs.iter_rows(named=True):
    u = UTIL_DISPLAY.get(row["sb.electric_utility"], row["sb.electric_utility"])
    bill = row["revenue_total"]
    cost = row["mc_total"] + row["residual_total"]
    bat = row["BAT_percustomer_total"]
    cs_bracket_rows.append({
        "utility": u, "x": 1.0, "xend": 2.0,
        "y": bill, "yend": cost, "clr": "#b71c1c",
    })
    cs_bat_rows.append({
        "utility": u, "x": 1.5,
        "y": max(bill, cost) + 100,
        "label": f"+${abs(bat):,.0f}/yr",
        "clr": "#b71c1c",
    })

cs_bracket_df = pl.DataFrame(cs_bracket_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(util_order))
)
cs_bat_df = pl.DataFrame(cs_bat_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(util_order))
)

(
    ggplot(cs_util_df, aes(x="bar", y="dollars", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.55)
    + geom_text(
        cs_util_totals, aes(x="bar", y="total", label="total_label"),
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=7, nudge_y=30, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_segment(
        cs_bracket_df,
        aes(x="x", xend="xend", y="y", yend="yend", color="clr"),
        size=1.0, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_text(
        cs_bat_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label", color="clr"),
        size=8, fontweight="bold", inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=CS_COMP_COLORS, breaks=CS_COMP_ORDER)
    + scale_color_identity()
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.18, 0))
    + facet_wrap("utility", ncol=4, scales="free_y")
    + labs(
        x="", y="$ / year",
        title="Typical HP customer: annual bill vs. cost of service by utility",
        fill="",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(16, 8),
        axis_text_x=element_text(size=8),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, fontweight="bold"),
    )
)
```


## Revenue requirement composition

Each utility's total residential revenue requirement — the amount it must
recover from residential ratepayers — breaks into three pieces:

1. **Delivery (Rate Case)** — the base T&D revenue requirement set in the
   utility's most recent rate case.
2. **Delivery Top-Ups** — additional delivery-side charges for policy programs
   (SBC, CES delivery, EV make-ready, DLM), DER credit recovery (VDER),
   and sunk-cost recovery (COVID arrears, late-payment waivers).
3. **Supply** — energy commodity, merchant function charge, and CES supply
   surcharge.

Data comes from the computed revenue requirement YAMLs in
`rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/rev_requirement/`.

```{python}
#| label: rev-req-data

RR_CONFIG_DIR = "rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/rev_requirement"
UTILITY_CONFIGS: dict[str, str] = {
    "CenHud": "cenhud.yaml",
    "ConEd": "coned.yaml",
    "NiMo": "nimo.yaml",
    "NYSEG": "nyseg.yaml",
    "RG&E": "rge.yaml",
    "O&R": "or.yaml",
    "PSEG-LI": "psegli.yaml",
}


def load_rev_requirements(
    configs: dict[str, str],
    ref: str,
    config_dir: str = RR_CONFIG_DIR,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Fetch utility revenue requirement YAMLs from RDP and build a DataFrame."""
    rows: list[dict] = []
    for display_name, filename in configs.items():
        content = fetch_rdp_file(f"{config_dir}/{filename}", ref=ref)
        cfg = yaml.safe_load(content)
        rows.append({
            "utility": display_name,
            "delivery_rr": cfg["delivery_revenue_requirement_from_rate_case"],
            "delivery_topups": cfg["delivery_revenue_requirement_topups"],
            "supply_rr": cfg["supply_revenue_requirement_topups"],
        })
    return pl.DataFrame(rows).with_columns(
        (pl.col("delivery_rr") + pl.col("delivery_topups") + pl.col("supply_rr"))
        .alias("total"),
    )


rr_data = load_rev_requirements(UTILITY_CONFIGS, ref=RDP_REF)

utility_order = rr_data.sort("total", descending=True)["utility"].to_list()

RR_COMPONENTS: dict[str, str] = {
    "delivery_rr": "Delivery (Rate Case)",
    "delivery_topups": "Delivery Top-Ups",
    "supply_rr": "Supply",
}
RR_COMPONENT_ORDER = list(RR_COMPONENTS.values())
RR_COLORS = {
    "Delivery (Rate Case)": "#56B4E9",
    "Delivery Top-Ups": "#E69F00",
    "Supply": "#999999",
}

rr_long = (
    rr_data.unpivot(
        on=["delivery_rr", "delivery_topups", "supply_rr"],
        index=["utility", "total"],
        variable_name="component_raw",
        value_name="dollars",
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("component_raw").replace_strict(RR_COMPONENTS).alias("component"),
        (pl.col("dollars") / pl.col("total") * 100).alias("pct"),
        (pl.col("dollars") / 1e9).alias("dollars_b"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)),
        pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(RR_COMPONENT_ORDER)),
    )
)

rr_totals = rr_data.with_columns(
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)),
    (pl.col("total") / 1e9).alias("total_b"),
    (pl.col("total") / 1e9)
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.1f}B", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("total_label"),
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rev-req-absolute
#| fig-cap: "Residential revenue requirement by utility ($B)"

(
    ggplot(rr_long, aes(x="utility", y="dollars_b", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.65)
    + geom_text(
        aes(x="utility", y="total_b", label="total_label"),
        data=rr_totals,
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=9, nudge_y=0.05, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=RR_COLORS, breaks=RR_COMPONENT_ORDER)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
    + labs(x="", y="Billions ($)", title="Residential revenue requirement by utility", fill="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 6))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rev-req-pct
#| fig-cap: "Revenue requirement composition (% of total)"

pct_labels = rr_long.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3).with_columns(
    pl.col("pct")
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"{v:.0f}%", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("pct_label"),
)

(
    ggplot(rr_long, aes(x="utility", y="pct", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.65)
    + geom_text(
        pct_labels,
        aes(label="pct_label"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        color="white", fontweight="bold", size=9,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=RR_COLORS, breaks=RR_COMPONENT_ORDER)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.02, 0))
    + labs(
        x="", y="% of total revenue requirement",
        title="Revenue requirement composition by utility",
        fill="",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 6))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: rev-req-table

rr_table = rr_data.select(
    "utility",
    (pl.col("delivery_rr") / 1e6).alias("delivery_rr_m"),
    (pl.col("delivery_topups") / 1e6).alias("delivery_topups_m"),
    (pl.col("supply_rr") / 1e6).alias("supply_rr_m"),
    (pl.col("total") / 1e6).alias("total_m"),
    (pl.col("delivery_topups") / pl.col("total")).alias("topups_pct"),
).sort("total_m", descending=True)

(
    GT(rr_table)
    .fmt_number(columns=["delivery_rr_m", "supply_rr_m", "total_m"], decimals=0)
    .fmt_number(columns="delivery_topups_m", decimals=1)
    .fmt_percent(columns="topups_pct", decimals=1)
    .cols_label(
        utility="Utility",
        delivery_rr_m="Delivery RR ($M)",
        delivery_topups_m="Delivery Top-Ups ($M)",
        supply_rr_m="Supply RR ($M)",
        total_m="Total ($M)",
        topups_pct="Top-Ups % of Total",
    )
)
```

### Revenue requirement vs. marginal cost allocation

The BAT decomposes each customer's cost of service into **marginal cost**
(economic burden: Σ load × MC) and **residual cost** (revenue requirement minus
total marginal costs, allocated per customer). Because we run paired CAIRO
simulations — delivery-only (run 1) and delivery+supply (run 2) — we can
decompose both marginal cost and residual cost into delivery and supply
components. This lets us compare the revenue requirement breakdown against the
BAT's cost allocation at a finer grain.

```{python}
#| label: bat-mc-residual-data

UTILITY_CODE_TO_DISPLAY: dict[str, str] = {
    v.replace(".yaml", ""): k for k, v in UTILITY_CONFIGS.items()
}

bat_enriched = (
    pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_12, hive_partitioning=True)
    .select(
        "sb.electric_utility", "weight",
        "economic_burden_delivery", "economic_burden_supply",
        "residual_share_delivery", "residual_share_supply",
        "annual_bill_delivery", "annual_bill_supply",
    )
    .collect()
)

mc_residual = (
    bat_enriched
    .group_by("sb.electric_utility")
    .agg(
        (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("mc_delivery"),
        (pl.col("economic_burden_supply") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("mc_supply"),
        (pl.col("residual_share_delivery") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("rs_delivery"),
        (pl.col("residual_share_supply") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("rs_supply"),
        (pl.col("annual_bill_delivery") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("bill_delivery"),
        (pl.col("annual_bill_supply") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("bill_supply"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("mc_delivery") + pl.col("mc_supply")).alias("mc_total"),
        (pl.col("rs_delivery") + pl.col("rs_supply")).alias("rs_total"),
        (pl.col("bill_delivery") + pl.col("bill_supply")).alias("bill_total"),
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility")
        .replace_strict(UTILITY_CODE_TO_DISPLAY)
        .alias("utility"),
    )
    .drop("sb.electric_utility")
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rr-vs-mc-residual
#| fig-cap: "Revenue requirement vs. marginal / residual cost allocation by utility"

BAR_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "rr": "Revenue\nRequirement",
    "mc": "Cost\nAllocation",
}
BAR_ORDER = list(BAR_LABELS.values())

CA_COMPONENTS = [
    "MC (Delivery)", "Residual (Delivery)",
    "MC (Supply)", "Residual (Supply)",
]
CA_COLORS = {
    "MC (Delivery)": "#1b7837",
    "Residual (Delivery)": "#a6dba0",
    "MC (Supply)": "#c51b7d",
    "Residual (Supply)": "#f1b6da",
}

ALL_COMPONENTS = ["Delivery (Rate Case)", "Delivery Top-Ups", "Supply"] + CA_COMPONENTS
ALL_COLORS = {**RR_COLORS, **CA_COLORS}

rr_for_join = rr_data.select(
    "utility",
    pl.col("delivery_rr"),
    pl.col("delivery_topups"),
    pl.col("supply_rr"),
    pl.col("total").alias("rr_total"),
)

combined = rr_for_join.join(mc_residual, on="utility")

records: list[dict] = []
for row in combined.iter_rows(named=True):
    u = row["utility"]
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["rr"], "component": "Delivery (Rate Case)", "dollars": row["delivery_rr"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["rr"], "component": "Delivery Top-Ups", "dollars": row["delivery_topups"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["rr"], "component": "Supply", "dollars": row["supply_rr"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["mc"], "component": "MC (Delivery)", "dollars": row["mc_delivery"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["mc"], "component": "Residual (Delivery)", "dollars": row["rs_delivery"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["mc"], "component": "MC (Supply)", "dollars": row["mc_supply"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["mc"], "component": "Residual (Supply)", "dollars": row["rs_supply"]})

plot_df = (
    pl.DataFrame(records)
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)),
        pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(BAR_ORDER)),
        pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(ALL_COMPONENTS)),
        (pl.col("dollars") / 1e9).alias("dollars_b"),
    )
)

(
    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bar", y="dollars_b", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.7)
    + scale_fill_manual(values=ALL_COLORS, breaks=ALL_COMPONENTS)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
    + facet_wrap("utility", ncol=4, scales="free_y")
    + labs(x="", y="Billions ($)", title="Revenue requirement vs. cost allocation", fill="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(16, 8),
        axis_text_x=element_text(size=8),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, fontweight="bold"),
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rr-vs-mc-residual-pct
#| fig-cap: "Revenue requirement vs. cost allocation (% of total)"

bar_totals = (
    plot_df.group_by("utility", "bar")
    .agg(pl.col("dollars").sum().alias("bar_total"))
)
plot_df_pct = (
    plot_df.join(bar_totals, on=["utility", "bar"])
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("dollars") / pl.col("bar_total") * 100).alias("pct"),
    )
    .drop("bar_total")
)

pct_labels_mc = plot_df_pct.filter(pl.col("pct").abs() >= 3).with_columns(
    pl.col("pct")
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"{v:.0f}%", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("pct_label"),
)

(
    ggplot(plot_df_pct, aes(x="bar", y="pct", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.7)
    + geom_text(
        pct_labels_mc,
        aes(label="pct_label"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        color="white", fontweight="bold", size=8,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=ALL_COLORS, breaks=ALL_COMPONENTS)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.02, 0))
    + facet_wrap("utility", ncol=4)
    + labs(x="", y="% of total", title="Revenue requirement vs. cost allocation (% of total)", fill="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(16, 8),
        axis_text_x=element_text(size=8),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, fontweight="bold"),
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: mc-residual-table

mc_table = (
    mc_residual
    .with_columns(pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)))
    .sort("utility")
    .select(
        "utility",
        (pl.col("mc_delivery") / 1e6).alias("mc_deliv"),
        (pl.col("rs_delivery") / 1e6).alias("resid_deliv"),
        (pl.col("mc_delivery") / pl.col("bill_delivery")).alias("mc_pct_deliv"),
        (pl.col("mc_supply") / 1e6).alias("mc_supply"),
        (pl.col("rs_supply") / 1e6).alias("resid_supply"),
        (pl.col("mc_supply") / pl.col("bill_supply")).alias("mc_pct_supply"),
        (pl.col("mc_total") / 1e6).alias("mc_total"),
        (pl.col("rs_total") / 1e6).alias("resid_total"),
        (pl.col("mc_total") / pl.col("bill_total")).alias("mc_pct_total"),
    )
)

(
    GT(mc_table)
    .fmt_number(
        columns=["mc_deliv", "resid_deliv", "mc_supply", "resid_supply", "mc_total", "resid_total"],
        decimals=0,
    )
    .fmt_percent(columns=["mc_pct_deliv", "mc_pct_supply", "mc_pct_total"], decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        utility="Utility",
        mc_deliv="MC Deliv ($M)",
        resid_deliv="Resid Deliv ($M)",
        mc_pct_deliv="MC % Deliv",
        mc_supply="MC Supply ($M)",
        resid_supply="Resid Supply ($M)",
        mc_pct_supply="MC % Supply",
        mc_total="MC Total ($M)",
        resid_total="Resid Total ($M)",
        mc_pct_total="MC % Total",
    )
    .tab_spanner(label="Delivery", columns=["mc_deliv", "resid_deliv", "mc_pct_deliv"])
    .tab_spanner(label="Supply", columns=["mc_supply", "resid_supply", "mc_pct_supply"])
    .tab_spanner(label="Total", columns=["mc_total", "resid_total", "mc_pct_total"])
)
```


## Heat pump seasonal rate

For each utility, we compare the current default flat rate against the proposed
HP seasonal rate. Both tariffs are calibrated URDB JSONs stored in
`rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/tariffs/electric/`. The delivery component
comes from the delivery-only file (`*_calibrated.json`), and supply is the
difference between the bundled file (`*_supply_calibrated.json`) and delivery.

```{python}
#| label: rate-table-data

import json

TARIFF_DIR = "rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/tariffs/electric"

TARIFF_UTILS: dict[str, str] = {
    "ConEd": "coned",
    "NiMo": "nimo",
    "NYSEG": "nyseg",
    "CenHud": "cenhud",
    "RG&E": "rge",
    "O&R": "or",
    "PSEG-LI": "psegli",
}


def _load_rate(filename: str) -> dict:
    content = fetch_rdp_file(f"{TARIFF_DIR}/{filename}", ref=RDP_REF)
    return json.loads(content)["items"][0]


def _get_rates(tariff: dict) -> list[float]:
    return [tier[0]["rate"] for tier in tariff["energyratestructure"]]


rate_rows: list[dict] = []
for display, code in TARIFF_UTILS.items():
    flat_d = _load_rate(f"{code}_flat_calibrated.json")
    flat_b = _load_rate(f"{code}_flat_supply_calibrated.json")
    seas_d = _load_rate(f"{code}_hp_seasonal_calibrated.json")
    seas_b = _load_rate(f"{code}_hp_seasonal_supply_calibrated.json")

    fd = _get_rates(flat_d)[0]
    fb = _get_rates(flat_b)[0]
    fs = fb - fd

    sd = _get_rates(seas_d)
    sb = _get_rates(seas_b)

    fixed = flat_d.get("fixedchargefirstmeter", 0)

    rate_rows.append({
        "utility": display,
        "rate": "Default",
        "delivery": fd * 100,
        "supply": fs * 100,
        "total": fb * 100,
        "discount": None,
    })
    rate_rows.append({
        "utility": display,
        "rate": "HP Summer",
        "delivery": sd[0] * 100,
        "supply": (sb[0] - sd[0]) * 100,
        "total": sb[0] * 100,
        "discount": (1 - sb[0] / fb),
    })
    rate_rows.append({
        "utility": display,
        "rate": "HP Winter",
        "delivery": sd[1] * 100,
        "supply": (sb[1] - sd[1]) * 100,
        "total": sb[1] * 100,
        "discount": (1 - sb[1] / fb),
    })

rate_table = pl.DataFrame(rate_rows)

_rate_gt = (
    GT(rate_table, rowname_col="rate", groupname_col="utility")
    .fmt_number(columns=["delivery", "supply", "total"], decimals=2)
    .fmt_percent(columns="discount", decimals=1)
    .sub_missing(columns="discount", missing_text="—")
    .cols_label(
        delivery="Delivery (¢/kWh)",
        supply="Supply (¢/kWh)",
        total="Total (¢/kWh)",
        discount="Discount",
    )
)

from pathlib import Path
Path("../cache").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
Path("../cache/rate_table.html").write_text(_rate_gt.as_raw_html())

_rate_gt
```

## BAT under HP seasonal rate

Does the HP seasonal rate actually fix the cross-subsidy? We compare the BAT
distributions under flat rates (run 1+2) and HP seasonal rates (run 5+6) — same
buildings, same upgrade 0, only the rate structure changes. If the seasonal rate
is well-designed, the HP overpayment should collapse to near zero.

```{python}
#| label: bat-56-ingest

bat_56 = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_56, hive_partitioning=True)

bat_all_56 = (
    bat_56
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight",
        "BAT_percustomer_total",
        HEATING_TYPE_COL,
        "sb.electric_utility",
    )
    .collect()
)
```

### ConEd

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-coned-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, ConEd (HP seasonal rate)"

bat_coned_56 = bat_all_56.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY)
plot_bat_histogram(bat_coned_56, title="BAT per-customer (total) — ConEd (HP seasonal rate)")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-coned-faceted-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd (HP seasonal rate)"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_coned_56, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd (HP seasonal rate)")
```

### Statewide

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-state-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, Statewide (HP seasonal rate)"

plot_bat_histogram(bat_all_56, title="BAT per-customer (total) — Statewide (HP seasonal rate)")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-state-faceted-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide (HP seasonal rate)"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_all_56, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide (HP seasonal rate)")
```

### Summary: HP overpayment before vs. after

```{python}
#| label: bat-comparison-table

col = "BAT_percustomer_total"
facet_dtype = pl.Enum(FACET_ORDER)

comparison_rows: list[dict] = []
for label, bat_df in [("Flat rate (status quo)", bat_all), ("HP seasonal rate", bat_all_56)]:
    labeled = bat_df.with_columns(
        pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
        .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
        .fill_null("Other")
        .alias("heating_label"),
    )
    for ht in FACET_ORDER:
        sub = labeled.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
        if len(sub) == 0:
            continue
        tw = sub["weight"].sum()
        pct_over = sub.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / tw * 100
        w_mean = _weighted_mean(sub, col, "weight")
        comparison_rows.append({
            "rate": label,
            "heating_type": ht,
            "mean_bat": w_mean,
            "pct_overpaying": pct_over / 100,
        })

bat_comp = pl.DataFrame(comparison_rows)

(
    GT(bat_comp, rowname_col="heating_type", groupname_col="rate")
    .fmt_currency(columns="mean_bat", decimals=0)
    .fmt_percent(columns="pct_overpaying", decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        mean_bat="Mean BAT ($/yr)",
        pct_overpaying="% Overpaying",
    )
)
```

## Bill impact of HP seasonal rate on HP customers

What happens to a fossil-fuel customer's total energy bill if they switch to a
heat pump **and** get the proposed HP seasonal electric rate? The "before" is
their current bill under today's flat rates (run 1+2, upgrade 0) and the
"after" is their bill as an HP customer on the seasonal rate (run 7+8,
upgrade 2). The delta captures both the fuel switch and the rate change.

```{python}
#| label: compute-deltas-switch-with-hprate

annual_before_switch = (
    bills_current
    .filter((pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH) & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY))
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_after_switch = (
    bills_hprate_hp
    .filter((pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH) & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY))
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_switch = annual_before_switch.join(annual_after_switch, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)

annual_before_switch_state = (
    bills_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_after_switch_state = (
    bills_hprate_hp
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_switch_state = annual_before_switch_state.join(annual_after_switch_state, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)
```

### ConEd — switching to HP with seasonal rate

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-switch-hprate
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — natural gas homes switching to HP with seasonal rate"

natgas_switch = (
    delta_switch
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas_switch)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane-switch-hprate
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — oil/propane homes switching to HP with seasonal rate"

oil_propane_switch = (
    delta_switch
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane_switch)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-elec-resistance-switch-hprate
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — electric resistance homes switching to HP with seasonal rate"

elec_resistance_switch = (
    delta_switch
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(elec_resistance_switch)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-all-switch-hprate
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary by heating fuel — switching to HP with seasonal rate (ConEd)"

non_hp_switch = (
    delta_switch
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_quadrant_bar([
    ("All non-HP", non_hp_switch),
    ("Natural gas", natgas_switch),
    ("Oil / propane", oil_propane_switch),
    ("Electric resistance", elec_resistance_switch),
])
```

### Statewide — switching to HP with seasonal rate

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — natural gas homes switching to HP with seasonal rate (Statewide)"

natgas_switch_state = (
    delta_switch_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas_switch_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-default-vs-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change comparison — natural gas homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate (Statewide)"

_comparison_rows = [("Default rate", natgas_state), ("HP seasonal rate", natgas_switch_state)]

_all_deltas = pl.concat([r[1] for r in _comparison_rows])
_snap = max(BIN_WIDTH, 50)
_x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(_all_deltas, "delta", "weight", 0.01) / _snap) * _snap
_x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(_all_deltas, "delta", "weight", 0.99) / _snap) * _snap

_parts = []
_label_records: list[dict] = []
for _label, _df in _comparison_rows:
    _binned = (
        _df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col("delta") / BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BIN_WIDTH + BIN_WIDTH / 2).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") <= -1000).then(pl.lit("savings > $1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") > -1000) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 0)).then(pl.lit("savings $0-1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") >= 0) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 1000)).then(pl.lit("losses $0-1k"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("losses > $1k"))
            .alias("quadrant"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "quadrant")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
        .with_columns(pl.lit(_label).alias("rate"))
    )
    _parts.append(_binned)

_global_max_w = max(
    float(p["weight_sum"].max()) for p in _parts if not p.is_empty()
)
_y_annot = _global_max_w * 1.05

for _label, _df in _comparison_rows:
    _pct = quadrant_pcts(_df)
    _q_bounds = [(_x_lo, -1000), (-1000, 0), (0, 1000), (1000, _x_hi)]
    _label_xs = [(max(lo, _x_lo) + min(hi, _x_hi)) / 2 for lo, hi in _q_bounds]
    for _q, _xp in zip(QUADRANT_ORDER, _label_xs):
        if _pct[_q] < 0.05:
            continue
        _label_records.append({
            "rate": _label, "x": _xp, "y": _y_annot,
            "text": f"{_q}\n{_pct[_q]:.1f}%", "color": QUADRANT_COLORS[_q],
        })

_rate_dtype = pl.Enum([r[0] for r in _comparison_rows])
_plot_df = pl.concat(_parts).with_columns(
    pl.col("quadrant").cast(pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)),
    pl.col("rate").cast(_rate_dtype),
)
_label_df = pl.DataFrame(_label_records).with_columns(pl.col("rate").cast(_rate_dtype))

(
    ggplot(_plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="quadrant"))
    + geom_col(width=BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=[-1000, 0, 1000], linetype="dotted", color="gray")
    + geom_text(
        _label_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="text", color="color"),
        inherit_aes=False, ha="center", size=9,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
    + scale_color_identity()
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(_x_lo, _x_hi))
    + facet_wrap("rate", ncol=1)
    + labs(x="Annual Bill Change ($)", y="# of Homes (weighted)")
    + guides(fill=False)
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(14, 8),
        strip_text=element_text(size=11, ha="left", weight="bold"),
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — oil/propane homes switching to HP with seasonal rate (Statewide)"

oil_propane_switch_state = (
    delta_switch_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane_switch_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-elec-resistance-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — electric resistance homes switching to HP with seasonal rate (Statewide)"

elec_resistance_switch_state = (
    delta_switch_state
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(elec_resistance_switch_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-natgas-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary — natural gas homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [
        ("Default rate", natgas_state),
        ("HP seasonal rate", natgas_switch_state),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-oil-propane-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary — oil/propane homes switching to HP with seasonal rate (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [("Oil / propane", oil_propane_switch_state)],
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("fuel oil", "#023047"),
        (" or ", "#000000"),
        ("propane", "#4a8fa0"),
        (" homes switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps with seasonal rate", "#fc9706"),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-all-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary by heating fuel — switching to HP with seasonal rate (Statewide)"

non_hp_switch_state = (
    delta_switch_state
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_quadrant_bar([
    ("All non-HP", non_hp_switch_state),
    ("Natural gas", natgas_switch_state),
    ("Oil / propane", oil_propane_switch_state),
    ("Electric resistance", elec_resistance_switch_state),
])
```

## Bill impact of HP seasonal rate on non-HP customers

What happens to fossil-fuel customers' bills if all existing HP customers opt
in to the seasonal rate — but the non-HP customers themselves stay on their
current heating system (upgrade 0) and on the recalibrated flat rate?

In run 5+6, the HP seasonal rate is applied to HP customers and the flat rate
is recalibrated for everyone else. Non-HP customers keep the same heating
system; what changes is a slight adjustment to the flat rate once the HP
cross-subsidy is removed. Comparing run 1+2 vs 5+6 for fossil-fuel buildings
shows this (expected to be very small) impact.

```{python}
#| label: compute-deltas-nonhp-rebal

annual_before_rebal = (
    bills_current
    .filter((pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH) & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY))
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_after_rebal = (
    bills_hprate_current
    .filter((pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH) & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY))
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_rebal = annual_before_rebal.join(annual_after_rebal, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)

annual_before_rebal_state = (
    bills_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_after_rebal_state = (
    bills_hprate_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_rebal_state = annual_before_rebal_state.join(annual_after_rebal_state, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)
```

### ConEd — non-HP bill impact of cross-subsidy removal

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-rebal
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — natural gas homes after cross-subsidy removal"

REBAL_BIN = 5
REBAL_TITLE = "Change in Non-HP Customer Bills after Cross-Subsidy Removal"

natgas_rebal = (
    delta_rebal
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas_rebal, bin_width=REBAL_BIN, title=REBAL_TITLE)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane-rebal
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — oil/propane homes after cross-subsidy removal"

oil_propane_rebal = (
    delta_rebal
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane_rebal, bin_width=REBAL_BIN, title=REBAL_TITLE)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-non-hp-rebal
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — all non-HP homes after cross-subsidy removal"

non_hp_rebal = (
    delta_rebal
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(non_hp_rebal, bin_width=REBAL_BIN, title=REBAL_TITLE)
```

### Statewide — non-HP bill impact of cross-subsidy removal

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-rebal-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — natural gas homes after cross-subsidy removal (Statewide)"

natgas_rebal_state = (
    delta_rebal_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas_rebal_state, bin_width=REBAL_BIN, title=REBAL_TITLE)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane-rebal-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — oil/propane homes after cross-subsidy removal (Statewide)"

oil_propane_rebal_state = (
    delta_rebal_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane_rebal_state, bin_width=REBAL_BIN, title=REBAL_TITLE)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-non-hp-rebal-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — all non-HP homes after cross-subsidy removal (Statewide)"

non_hp_rebal_state = (
    delta_rebal_state
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(non_hp_rebal_state, bin_width=REBAL_BIN, title=REBAL_TITLE)
```
